mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in the federal funds rate over recent months signals an accommodative monetary environment that reduces the equity discount rate and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Twenty-day forward returns are not significantly positive during months with declining FEDFUNDS, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market slack that crushes household income and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns are positive in greater than fifty percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and contraction in industrial production indicates a demand-supply imbalance that compresses corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and falling INDPRO fails to produce negative twenty-day returns in more than half of triggered periods, or Rank IC turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated and the policy rate is near zero, upward pressure in consumer prices forces anticipated monetary tightening that raises real discount rates and produces negative short-term equity returns.
regime_hint: elevated inflation with near-zero policy rates
failure_condition: Positive five-day forward returns occur in more than fifty-five percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220 and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor income that feeds into aggregate consumption and top-line revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between PAYEMS changes and twenty-day forward returns breaks down with a rolling two-year Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below a critical threshold indicates a contraction in real economic output that reduces corporate earnings capacity and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 sixty-day forward returns are non-negative in more than fifty percent of instances when INDPRO is below 95.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy and rising unemployment creates a dual drag on equities through higher discount rates and weaker earnings expectations, producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS above 0.5 and UNRATE above 8.0 coincides with positive five-day returns in more than fifty percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below a key level signals weakness in construction activity and related durable goods demand that weighs on cyclical earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Twenty-day forward returns are positive in greater than fifty percent of months when HOUST is below 600 thousand units.
